summary: Draft PR matching one game-mode/debug-results function: gm_801B0DD0 in src/melee/gm/gm_1B03.c. The implementation loads cached debug/sound-test match parameters from un_803FA258 into DebugResultsData, rebuilds MatchEnd standings with gm_80166A98, derives a character victory-fanfare bitmask from non-CPU/valid non-big-loser standings, plays result audio setup calls, then advances results initialization. The accompanying static header replaces a raw 0x2284-byte pad for DebugResultsData with the fields needed by the matched function plus a MatchEnd member. decomp.dev reported 1 new match, +332 bytes, and overall matched code 66.99% (+0.01%).

key_files.reason: Replaced the nonmatching marker for gm_801B0DD0 with a matched C implementation.
Defined the beginning of DebugResultsData as bitfields/bytes and embedded MatchEnd instead of treating it as an opaque pad.

what_changed: Implemented gm_801B0DD0(MinorScene* arg0), matching it at 100% according to the PR body and decomp.dev report.
gm_801B0DD0 retrieves struct DebugResultsData via gm_801A427C(arg0) and takes MatchEnd* match_end from data->match_end.
Copied cached parameter values from un_803FA258 indices 0x5E, 0x5F, and 0x5A through 0x5D into DebugResultsData fields x0_0, x0_1, x1, x2, x3, and x4.
Called gm_80166A98 with values from un_803FA258 indices 0x60, 0x51 through 0x54, and 0x56 through 0x59, including casts to s8 and subtracting 1 from four character/slot-like values.
Looped over four player_standings entries, skipping slot_type == 3 and is_big_loser != 0, and ORed lbAudioAx_80026E84(character_kind) into a u64 sfx_result mask.
Called lbAudioAx_80026F2C(0x14), lbAudioAx_8002702C(0x4, sfx_result), gm_80168FC4(), gm_801701A0(), and PAD_STACK(8).
Changed DebugResultsData from u8 pad_x0[0x2284] to named fields: x0_0:1, x0_1:1, x0_other:6, x1, x2, x3, x4, pad_x5[3], and MatchEnd match_end.
smart_moves: Used a narrow partial struct definition for DebugResultsData: only the accessed prefix and MatchEnd member were named, leaving unknown bits as x0_other and padding as pad_x5.
Represented the first byte as bitfields x0_0, x0_1, and x0_other, matching stores from un_803FA258[0x5E] and [0x5F] without inventing semantic names.
Kept generated/unknown symbol names such as gm_801B0DD0, un_803FA258, and x0_0 because the PR evidence does not establish better names.
Built the result fanfare as a u64 OR mask, consistent with lbAudioAx_80026E84 returning per-character audio bits and lbAudioAx_8002702C consuming the combined mask.
Preserved casts and arithmetic seen in the matched call shape: (s8) for several un_803FA258 values and -1 adjustment for four later arguments.
decomp_lessons: For debug/results code, cached global arrays can often be mapped directly into a partially recovered scene-data struct before the broader struct is understood.
When a matched function only needs a few fields, replacing a large opaque pad with a minimal prefix plus known embedded struct can unlock matching while avoiding overconfident naming.
Bitfield layout may be necessary when consecutive assignments target individual low bits of an initial byte; keep the remaining bits explicitly reserved.
Audio result setup can be expressed as a mask accumulation over player standings, with filtering based on slot_type and is_big_loser before passing character_kind to audio helpers.
For progress PRs with many attempted functions, rely on the matched table and decomp.dev report for confirmed matches; the failed list is not evidence of implemented changes.
naming_conventions: Unknown game-mode functions remain address-based with gm_ prefix, e.g. gm_801B0DD0, gm_80166A98, gm_80168FC4, gm_801701A0.
Known structs use CamelCase type names such as MinorScene, MatchEnd, and DebugResultsData.
Partially known struct fields use offset-like names such as x0_0, x0_1, x0_other, x1, x2, x3, x4, and pad_x5.
Audio helper symbols keep the lbAudioAx_ address-based naming style, e.g. lbAudioAx_80026E84, lbAudioAx_80026F2C, and lbAudioAx_8002702C.
Global unknown data remains address-based with un_ prefix, e.g. un_803FA258.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used u64 sfx_result initialized to 0 so OR accumulation matches the result-audio bitmask behavior.
Declared int i for the four-player loop rather than embedding loop-specific assumptions into the type.
Matched player filtering with a compound condition: slot_type != 3 and is_big_loser == 0.
Kept exact constants from the diff: un_803FA258 indices 0x51-0x60, lbAudioAx_80026F2C argument 0x14, lbAudioAx_8002702C first argument 0x4, and PAD_STACK(8).
Placed MatchEnd immediately after pad_x5[3] in DebugResultsData, aligning the recovered prefix to the match_end member used by gm_801B0DD0.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code 66.99% (+0.01%, +332 bytes) and listed gm_801B0DD0 as a new 100.00% match in main/melee/gm/gm_1B03.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename DebugResultsData fields x0_0, x0_1, x1-x4, or x0_other.
Search for other gm_1B03.c results-screen functions that use un_803FA258 cached debug menu values.
Look for MatchEnd/player_standings struct refinements involving slot_type, is_big_loser, and character_kind.
Search for lbAudioAx_80026E84 or lbAudioAx_8002702C usage to confirm result fanfare bitmask conventions.
